title: Minimum cost to cut the stick| (DP-50)
content:
Problem Description: We are given a stick of length N and a cuts array of size C. The stick has markings as shown, and the cuts array depicts the marking at which the stick needs to be cut (shown in red).

Note: It is necessary to make cuts at all the red markings.

Whenever we make a cut, we incur a cost. This cost is equal to the length of the stick on which we are making the cut.

We need to find the minimum cost incurred to cut the stick at all the cut points.

Examples 
Input: n = 7, cuts = [1, 3, 4, 5]
Output: 16
Explanation: Rearranging the cuts to be [3, 5, 1, 4] for example will lead to a scenario with total cost = 16



Input: n = 7, cuts = [1, 3, 6]
Output: 14
Explanation: The optimal order for cutting the stick is [3, 1, 6].
The cost will be => 7 + 3 + 4 => 14.

Disclaimer: Here is the practice link to help you assess your knowledge better. It's highly recommend trying to solve it before looking at the solution.

Recursion
Algorithm
We need to minimize the cost incurred to cut the stick. Whenever we make a cut, we are changing the length of the stick which in turn decides the cost. Therefore the order in which we make the cuts changes the total cost. As discussed in DP-48 whenever the order of solving the problem comes into play, we can think in terms of Partition DP.

We are given a stick of size N and the cuts array. Every element of the cuts array represents the marking at which one cut needs to be made. When we make a cut, we are dividing the stick into two different parts.

Ideally, we would want to place the i, and j pointers at both ends of the cuts array and try to solve the problem recursively, which we will eventually do. But before that we need to sort the cuts array. By sorting the cuts array, we know that at whatever point we are making the cut, the information on the markings of the left portion will always be present on the left side of the cuts array partition. Similarly on the right side.
  • Consider the stick as a segment defined by two boundaries (start and end positions). If there are no cuts to be made between these boundaries, the cost is zero.
  • For each cut, calculate:
    The cost of cutting the current segment (length of the segment).
    The cost of recursively cutting the left sub-segment (from start to the current cut).
    The cost of recursively cutting the right sub-segment (from the current cut to the end).
  • Sum these costs to get the total cost for that choice of cut. Choose the cut position that gives the minimum total cost.
Code
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849505152535455
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick
class Solution {
public:
    // Recursive function to find the minimum cost to cut between indices i and j in cuts
    int findMinimumCost(int i, int j, vector<int> &cuts) {
        // Base case: no cuts to consider
        if (i > j) {
            return 0;
        }

        int mini = INT_MAX;

        // Try cutting at every position between i and j
        for (int ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
            // Cost of making the current cut plus
            // cost of cutting left and right sub-segments recursively
            int ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] +
                      findMinimumCost(i, ind - 1, cuts) +
                      findMinimumCost(ind + 1, j, cuts);

            // Update minimum cost
            mini = min(mini, ans);
        }

        return mini;
    }

    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick
    int minimumCost(int n, int c, vector<int> &cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts array
        cuts.push_back(n);
        cuts.insert(cuts.begin(), 0);

        // Sort the cuts array
        sort(cuts.begin(), cuts.end());

        // Compute the minimum cost to make all cuts between indices 1 and c
        return findMinimumCost(1, c, cuts);
    }
};

int main() {
    vector<int> cuts = {3, 5, 1, 4};
    int c = cuts.size();
    int n = 7;

    Solution sol;
    cout << "The minimum cost incurred is: " << sol.minimumCost(n, c, cuts) << endl;

    return 0;
}
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950
import java.util.*;

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick
class Solution {
    // Recursive function to find the minimum cost to cut between indices i and j in cuts
    public int findMinimumCost(int i, int j, List<Integer> cuts) {
        // Base case: no cuts to consider
        if (i > j) {
            return 0;
        }

        int mini = Integer.MAX_VALUE;

        // Try cutting at every position between i and j
        for (int ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
            // Cost of making the current cut plus
            // cost of cutting left and right sub-segments recursively
            int ans = cuts.get(j + 1) - cuts.get(i - 1) +
                      findMinimumCost(i, ind - 1, cuts) +
                      findMinimumCost(ind + 1, j, cuts);

            // Update minimum cost
            mini = Math.min(mini, ans);
        }

        return mini;
    }

    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick
    public int minimumCost(int n, List<Integer> cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts list
        cuts.add(n);
        cuts.add(0);
        Collections.sort(cuts);

        // Compute the minimum cost to make all cuts between indices 1 and cuts.size()-2
        return findMinimumCost(1, cuts.size() - 2, cuts);
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        List<Integer> cuts = new ArrayList<>(Arrays.asList(3, 5, 1, 4));
        int n = 7;

        Solution sol = new Solution();
        System.out.println("The minimum cost incurred is: " + sol.minimumCost(n, cuts));
    }
}

# Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick
class Solution:
    # Recursive function to find the minimum cost to cut between indices i and j in cuts
    def findMinimumCost(self, i, j, cuts):
        # Base case: no cuts to consider
        if i > j:
            return 0

        mini = float('inf')

        # Try cutting at every position between i and j
        for ind in range(i, j + 1):
            # Cost of making the current cut plus
            # cost of cutting left and right sub-segments recursively
            ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] + \
                  self.findMinimumCost(i, ind - 1, cuts) + \
                  self.findMinimumCost(ind + 1, j, cuts)

            # Update minimum cost
            mini = min(mini, ans)

        return mini

    # Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick
    def minimumCost(self, n, cuts):
        # Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts list
        cuts.append(n)
        cuts.insert(0, 0)
        cuts.sort()

        # Compute the minimum cost to make all cuts between indices 1 and len(cuts)-2
        return self.findMinimumCost(1, len(cuts) - 2, cuts)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    cuts = [3, 5, 1, 4]
    n = 7

    sol = Solution()
    print("The minimum cost incurred is:", sol.minimumCost(n, cuts))

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick
class Solution {
    // Recursive function to find the minimum cost to cut between indices i and j in cuts
    findMinimumCost(i, j, cuts) {
        // Base case: no cuts to consider
        if (i > j) {
            return 0;
        }

        let mini = Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER;

        // Try cutting at every position between i and j
        for (let ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
            // Cost of making the current cut plus
            // cost of cutting left and right sub-segments recursively
            let ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] +
                      this.findMinimumCost(i, ind - 1, cuts) +
                      this.findMinimumCost(ind + 1, j, cuts);

            // Update minimum cost
            mini = Math.min(mini, ans);
        }

        return mini;
    }

    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick
    minimumCost(n, cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts array
        cuts.push(n);
        cuts.unshift(0);
        cuts.sort((a, b) => a - b);

        // Compute the minimum cost to make all cuts between indices 1 and cuts.length-2
        return this.findMinimumCost(1, cuts.length - 2, cuts);
    }
}

// Main execution
const cuts = [3, 5, 1, 4];
const n = 7;
const sol = new Solution();

console.log("The minimum cost incurred is:", sol.minimumCost(n, cuts));
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(2^C), as we try all possible cuts between the current boundary, the time complexity is exponential.
Space Complexity: O(C), additional space used for recursive stack.

Memoization
Algorithm
To optimize our time complexity we can memoize our solution to prevent repeated computation of overlapping sub problems. Steps to memoize our solution:
  • Create a dp array of size [c+1][c+1] as i and j can range from 0 to c. Initialize the dp array to -1.
  • For a subproblem from i to j, if its result is already computed, return the result.
  • If the result is yet not computed, compute the result using recursive solution and store it in dp array for future.
Code
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick using memoization
class Solution {
public:
    // Recursive function with memoization to find minimum cost between indices i and j in cuts
    int findMinimumCost(int i, int j, vector<int> &cuts, vector<vector<int>> &dp) {
        // Base case: no cuts to consider
        if (i > j) {
            return 0;
        }

        // Return precomputed result if available
        if (dp[i][j] != -1) {
            return dp[i][j];
        }

        int mini = INT_MAX;

        // Try cutting at every position between i and j
        for (int ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
            // Calculate cost of current cut plus costs of left and right subproblems
            int ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] +
                      findMinimumCost(i, ind - 1, cuts, dp) +
                      findMinimumCost(ind + 1, j, cuts, dp);

            // Update minimum cost
            mini = min(mini, ans);
        }

        // Store and return the minimum cost found
        return dp[i][j] = mini;
    }

    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick
    int minimumCost(int n, int c, vector<int> &cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts array
        cuts.push_back(n);
        cuts.insert(cuts.begin(), 0);

        // Sort the cuts array
        sort(cuts.begin(), cuts.end());

        // Create a DP table initialized with -1
        vector<vector<int>> dp(c + 1, vector<int>(c + 1, -1));

        // Compute minimum cost to make all cuts between indices 1 and c
        return findMinimumCost(1, c, cuts, dp);
    }
};

int main() {
    vector<int> cuts = {3, 5, 1, 4};
    int c = cuts.size();
    int n = 7;

    Solution sol;
    cout << "The minimum cost incurred is: " << sol.minimumCost(n, c, cuts) << endl;

    return 0;
}
123456789101112131415161718192021222324252627282930313233343536373839404142434445464748495051525354555657585960616263
import java.util.*;

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick using memoization
class Solution {
    // Recursive function with memoization to find minimum cost between indices i and j in cuts
    public int findMinimumCost(int i, int j, List<Integer> cuts, int[][] dp) {
        // Base case: no cuts to consider
        if (i > j) {
            return 0;
        }

        // Return precomputed result if available
        if (dp[i][j] != -1) {
            return dp[i][j];
        }

        int mini = Integer.MAX_VALUE;

        // Try cutting at every position between i and j
        for (int ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
            // Cost of making current cut plus left and right subproblems
            int ans = cuts.get(j + 1) - cuts.get(i - 1) +
                      findMinimumCost(i, ind - 1, cuts, dp) +
                      findMinimumCost(ind + 1, j, cuts, dp);

            // Update minimum cost
            mini = Math.min(mini, ans);
        }

        // Store and return minimum cost found
        return dp[i][j] = mini;
    }

    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick
    public int minimumCost(int n, List<Integer> cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts list
        cuts.add(n);
        cuts.add(0);
        Collections.sort(cuts);

        int c = cuts.size() - 2; // number of cuts excluding ends

        // Create DP table initialized with -1
        int[][] dp = new int[c + 2][c + 2];
        for (int[] row : dp) {
            Arrays.fill(row, -1);
        }

        // Compute minimum cost to make all cuts between indices 1 and c
        return findMinimumCost(1, c, cuts, dp);
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        List<Integer> cuts = new ArrayList<>(Arrays.asList(3, 5, 1, 4));
        int n = 7;

        Solution sol = new Solution();
        System.out.println("The minimum cost incurred is: " + sol.minimumCost(n, cuts));
    }
}

# Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick using memoization
class Solution:
    # Recursive function with memoization to find minimum cost between indices i and j in cuts
    def findMinimumCost(self, i, j, cuts, dp):
        # Base case: no cuts to consider
        if i > j:
            return 0

        # Return precomputed result if available
        if dp[i][j] != -1:
            return dp[i][j]

        mini = float('inf')

        # Try cutting at every position between i and j
        for ind in range(i, j + 1):
            # Cost of making current cut plus left and right subproblems
            ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] + \
                  self.findMinimumCost(i, ind - 1, cuts, dp) + \
                  self.findMinimumCost(ind + 1, j, cuts, dp)

            # Update minimum cost
            mini = min(mini, ans)

        # Store and return minimum cost found
        dp[i][j] = mini
        return mini

    # Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick
    def minimumCost(self, n, cuts):
        # Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts list
        cuts.append(n)
        cuts.insert(0, 0)
        cuts.sort()

        c = len(cuts) - 2  # number of cuts excluding ends

        # Create DP table initialized with -1
        dp = [[-1] * (c + 2) for _ in range(c + 2)]

        # Compute minimum cost to make all cuts between indices 1 and c
        return self.findMinimumCost(1, c, cuts, dp)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    cuts = [3, 5, 1, 4]
    n = 7

    sol = Solution()
    print("The minimum cost incurred is:", sol.minimumCost(n, cuts))

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick using memoization
class Solution {
    // Recursive function with memoization to find minimum cost between indices i and j in cuts
    findMinimumCost(i, j, cuts, dp) {
        // Base case: no cuts to consider
        if (i > j) {
            return 0;
        }

        // Return precomputed result if available
        if (dp[i][j] !== -1) {
            return dp[i][j];
        }

        let mini = Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER;

        // Try cutting at every position between i and j
        for (let ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
            // Cost of making current cut plus left and right subproblems
            let ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] +
                      this.findMinimumCost(i, ind - 1, cuts, dp) +
                      this.findMinimumCost(ind + 1, j, cuts, dp);

            // Update minimum cost
            mini = Math.min(mini, ans);
        }

        // Store and return minimum cost found
        dp[i][j] = mini;
        return mini;
    }

    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick
    minimumCost(n, cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts array
        cuts.push(n);
        cuts.unshift(0);
        cuts.sort((a, b) => a - b);

        const c = cuts.length - 2; // number of cuts excluding ends

        // Create DP table initialized with -1
        const dp = Array.from({ length: c + 2 }, () => Array(c + 2).fill(-1));

        // Compute minimum cost to make all cuts between indices 1 and c
        return this.findMinimumCost(1, c, cuts, dp);
    }
}

// Main execution
const cuts = [3, 5, 1, 4];
const n = 7;
const sol = new Solution();

console.log("The minimum cost incurred is:", sol.minimumCost(n, cuts));
Complexity Analysis Time Complexity: O(C^3), for every state in our dp table i.e. c^2 states, we try all possible cuts between i and j.
Space Complexity: O(C^2), additional space used for dp array and recursion stack.

Tabulation
Algorithm
To optimize our solution even further, we can convert our recursive solution into tabulation approach to prevent the extra space being used for recursive stack. To convert our recursive solution into tabulation approach we need to follow these steps:
  • First of all, we handle the base case. If (i > j) we return 0. To cover this case we can initialize the entire dp array with 0.
  • Next, memoization is a top-down approach, whereas tabulation is bottom-up. Our changing parameters i and j will change in opposite directions, i.e i will change from c to 1 and j will change from 1 to c.
  • Next, we copy down the recursive logic inside the nested loops.
Code
12345678910111213141516171819202122232425262728293031323334353637383940414243444546474849505152535455
#include <bits/stdc++.h>
using namespace std;

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick using tabulation (bottom-up DP)
class Solution {
public:
    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick using tabulation
    int minimumCost(int n, int c, vector<int> &cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts array
        cuts.push_back(n);
        cuts.insert(cuts.begin(), 0);

        // Sort the cuts array
        sort(cuts.begin(), cuts.end());

        // Create a DP table initialized with 0
        vector<vector<int>> dp(c + 2, vector<int>(c + 2, 0));

        // Fill the DP table for all possible segments
        // i traverses backward from c to 1
        for (int i = c; i >= 1; i--) {
            // j traverses forward from i to c
            for (int j = i; j <= c; j++) {
                int mini = INT_MAX;

                // Try making a cut at every position between i and j
                for (int ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
                    // Cost of making the current cut plus costs of left and right subproblems
                    int ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] + dp[i][ind - 1] + dp[ind + 1][j];

                    // Update minimum cost
                    mini = min(mini, ans);
                }

                // Store the minimum cost in DP table
                dp[i][j] = mini;
            }
        }

        // The answer is the minimum cost to cut between indices 1 and c
        return dp[1][c];
    }
};

int main() {
    vector<int> cuts = {3, 5, 1, 4};
    int c = cuts.size();
    int n = 7;

    Solution sol;
    cout << "The minimum cost incurred is: " << sol.minimumCost(n, c, cuts) << endl;

    return 0;
}
1234567891011121314151617181920212223242526272829303132333435363738394041424344454647484950
import java.util.*;

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick using tabulation (bottom-up DP)
class Solution {
    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick using tabulation
    public int minimumCost(int n, List<Integer> cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts list
        cuts.add(n);
        cuts.add(0);
        Collections.sort(cuts);

        int c = cuts.size() - 2; // number of cuts excluding ends

        // Create DP table initialized with 0
        int[][] dp = new int[c + 2][c + 2];

        // Fill DP table for all segments
        for (int i = c; i >= 1; i--) {
            for (int j = i; j <= c; j++) {
                int mini = Integer.MAX_VALUE;

                // Try making a cut at every position between i and j
                for (int ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
                    // Cost of current cut plus left and right subproblems
                    int ans = cuts.get(j + 1) - cuts.get(i - 1) + dp[i][ind - 1] + dp[ind + 1][j];

                    // Update minimum cost
                    mini = Math.min(mini, ans);
                }

                // Store minimum cost in DP table
                dp[i][j] = mini;
            }
        }

        // Return minimum cost to cut between indices 1 and c
        return dp[1][c];
    }
}

public class Main {
    public static void main(String[] args) {
        List<Integer> cuts = new ArrayList<>(Arrays.asList(3, 5, 1, 4));
        int n = 7;

        Solution sol = new Solution();
        System.out.println("The minimum cost incurred is: " + sol.minimumCost(n, cuts));
    }
}

# Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick using tabulation (bottom-up DP)
class Solution:
    # Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick using tabulation
    def minimumCost(self, n, cuts):
        # Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts list
        cuts.append(n)
        cuts.insert(0, 0)
        cuts.sort()

        c = len(cuts) - 2  # number of cuts excluding ends

        # Create DP table initialized with 0
        dp = [[0] * (c + 2) for _ in range(c + 2)]

        # Fill DP table for all segments
        for i in range(c, 0, -1):
            for j in range(i, c + 1):
                mini = float('inf')

                # Try making a cut at every position between i and j
                for ind in range(i, j + 1):
                    # Cost of current cut plus left and right subproblems
                    ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] + dp[i][ind - 1] + dp[ind + 1][j]

                    # Update minimum cost
                    mini = min(mini, ans)

                # Store minimum cost in DP table
                dp[i][j] = mini

        # Return minimum cost to cut between indices 1 and c
        return dp[1][c]


if __name__ == "__main__":
    cuts = [3, 5, 1, 4]
    n = 7

    sol = Solution()
    print("The minimum cost incurred is:", sol.minimumCost(n, cuts))

// Solution class to compute minimum cost to cut a stick using tabulation (bottom-up DP)
class Solution {
    // Function to calculate minimum total cost to cut the stick using tabulation
    minimumCost(n, cuts) {
        // Add the two ends of the stick to the cuts array
        cuts.push(n);
        cuts.unshift(0);
        cuts.sort((a, b) => a - b);

        const c = cuts.length - 2; // number of cuts excluding ends

        // Create DP table initialized with 0
        const dp = Array.from({ length: c + 2 }, () => Array(c + 2).fill(0));

        // Fill DP table for all segments
        for (let i = c; i >= 1; i--) {
            for (let j = i; j <= c; j++) {
                let mini = Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER;

                // Try making a cut at every position between i and j
                for (let ind = i; ind <= j; ind++) {
                    // Cost of current cut plus left and right subproblems
                    let ans = cuts[j + 1] - cuts[i - 1] + dp[i][ind - 1] + dp[ind + 1][j];

                    // Update minimum cost
                    mini = Math.min(mini, ans);
                }

                // Store minimum cost in DP table
                dp[i][j] = mini;
            }
        }

        // Return minimum cost to cut between indices 1 and c
        return dp[1][c];
    }
}

// Main execution

const n = 7;
